records.ety: [AS. ābīdan; pref. ā- (cf. Goth. us-, G. er-, orig. meaning out) + bīdan to bide. See Bide.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Abode (<?/), formerly Abid (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Abiding (<?/).]

defs: To wait; to pause; to delay.
To stay; to continue in a place; to have one's abode; to dwell; to sojourn; -- with with before a person, and commonly with at or in before a place.
quotes: Let the damsel abide with us a few days.

defs: To remain stable or fixed in some state or condition; to continue; to remain.
quotes: Let every man abide in the same calling.
The poor fellow was obstinate enough to abide by what he said at first.

defs: To wait for; to be prepared for; to await; to watch for; as, I abide my time.
To endure; to sustain; to submit to.
quotes: [Thou] shalt abide her judgment on it.

defs: To bear patiently; to tolerate; to put up with.
quotes: She could not abide Master Shallow.

defs: To stand the consequences of; to answer for; to suffer for.
quotes: Dearly I abide that boast so vain.
notes: [Confused with aby to pay for. See Aby.]
